counters.description: belveth is an extremely hard matchup, even in the hands of the best karthus players. i recommend banning belveth to anyone looking to play/main karthus.

although i don't recommend banning rengar to anyone below challenger, generally speaking he is a strong counter to karthus. his empowered w is very strong against karthus who is primarily a burst champion. hard matchup.

deceptively hard matchup due to the nature of each champions playstyles. ivern is not weak early game, clears camps extremely quickly (not vulnerable to karthus lv4 invades), and daisy makes him a formidable 1v1 dueler against karthus

udyr is extremely strong in this meta, and a good udyr player will bring karthus down. his w provides so much sustain and alongside movement speed buffs and slows from phoenix form, it's hard to play.

although evelynn this current patch (14.20) is not very strong, if you continue to play karthus be wary of her in the future. historically she is the best ap counter to karthus in the jungle due to her being able to ult to dodge karthus r and high ap stealth regen ratios.

synergies.description: any ad mid is a good synergy, but assassins that aren't hard countered by zhonyas and have tools for extended skirmishing are best.
leona has cc that doesn't move the enemy champion (the way an alistar/naut will) that might make you miss q's in uncoordinated play.
similar to qiyana, he scales extremely well, can act as an assassin and bruiser, deals ad damage, and can even split push! all things complimentary to karthus.
rell is my favorite support champ to play with in league. multiple ways to crowd control, snap engage from range, and holding people in place for karthus q in her ultimate. perfect support.

thresh requires a bit more skill from both the karthus player and the thresh, but when in synch it's a great combo. thresh has a lot of layered cc, heavy slows that synergize well as well as his lantern that mobilizes an otherwise immobile karthus.
